items.reason: The obsolete standalone client DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT entry was removed, matching the new worker-authoritative sampling model.
The dscli worker parameter table replaces log_rate_limit with request/access/diagnostic sample rates and documents derivation.
The context index now routes log-sampling design questions to the new log-sampler-design document.
The logging module overview adds LogSampler and points AccessRecorder behavior to the sampler design.
The Sticky Explicit section says a later request-only update keeps access=0.3, but the implementation/test show ever-explicit blocks derivation and omitted access/diagnostic fall back to 1.0.
It says later request updates must not overwrite an explicit parameter, but BuildAndPublishSnapshot and DerivationPersistentExplicitBlocks show omitted ever-explicit rates become 1.0.
AccessRecorder gained Object/Stream/RequestOut facades, lazy sampled-out skips, and sampler-owned prechecks, but this lifecycle/reference doc was not updated.
It still frames PLOG around log_rate_limit/LogRateLimiter bypass even though the PR removes log_rate_limit and routes request PLOG through diagnostic_sample_rate supplement sampling.
It still states PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit request sampling and only bypasses the rate-limiter decision, which is stale under the new LogSampler behavior.
